raw_response: {"rating": -0.5, "lean": "Center", "explanation": "This is a news/informational report — specifically a public health explainer written by an academic author. The piece is primarily focused on health and safety guidance with minimal political framing. One minor directional element (reference to a warming climate as a growing concern) introduces a slight left-leaning signal, but the overall content is overwhelmingly apolitical and practical, placing it at Center. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author identifies as someone who studies 'health risks in a warming climate,' which implicitly accepts the scientific and policy framing associated with climate change — a topic that carries mild left-leaning valence in contemporary political discourse, though the reference is brief and not advocacy-oriented; (2) The piece notes that northern cities are opening cooling centers 'as heat waves occur there more frequently than they did in the past,' which subtly frames climate-linked weather changes as an accepted premise, consistent with a slight left-leaning environmental framing; (3) Beyond these two minor signals, the article contains no political advocacy, no partisan framing, no ideological language, and no policy recommendations — the vast majority of the content is health guidance applicable to all readers regardless of political affiliation; (4) The author draws on CDC and National Weather Service sources, which are mainstream institutional references that do not themselves introduce political bias in this context."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is a news/informational report — specifically a public health explainer written by an academic author. The piece is primarily focused on health and safety guidance with minimal political framing. One minor directional element (reference to a warming climate as a growing concern) introduces a slight left-leaning signal, but the overall content is overwhelmingly apolitical and practical, placing it at Center. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author identifies as someone who studies 'health risks in a warming climate,' which implicitly accepts the scientific and policy framing associated with climate change — a topic that carries mild left-leaning valence in contemporary political discourse, though the reference is brief and not advocacy-oriented; (2) The piece notes that northern cities are opening cooling centers 'as heat waves occur there more frequently than they did in the past,' which subtly frames climate-linked weather changes as an accepted premise, consistent with a slight left-leaning environmental framing; (3) Beyond these two minor signals, the article contains no political advocacy, no partisan framing, no ideological language, and no policy recommendations — the vast majority of the content is health guidance applicable to all readers regardless of political affiliation; (4) The author draws on CDC and National Weather Service sources, which are mainstream institutional references that do not themselves introduce political bias in this context.
